name: synapse
description: A live project node map that any LLM installed in VS Code can read and update — components, features, decisions, dependencies, todos and problems tracked as an interactive graph.

synapse.autoOpen.description: Automatically open the Synapse canvas when a project with an existing map is opened in VS Code.

walkthroughs.title: Set up Synapse
walkthroughs.description: Connect your LLM to the Synapse project map

steps.title: What Synapse does
steps.description:
Synapse maintains a live node graph of your project — components, features, decisions, dependencies, todos and problems. Your LLM reads and updates it automatically so every session starts with full context.

Open the canvas any time with **Ctrl+Shift+Y**.

steps.title: Connect your LLM
steps.description:
Synapse auto-detects LLMs installed in VS Code and writes the MCP configuration for each one. If auto-detection missed yours, follow the manual steps below.

[Open Manual Setup Guide](command:synapse.walkthrough)

steps.title: Open your first canvas
steps.description:
Press **Ctrl+Shift+Y** or run **Synapse: Open Canvas** from the command palette.

[Open Canvas](command:synapse.open)

steps.title: Ask your LLM to map the project
steps.description:
In your LLM terminal/chat, run:

```
Use the Synapse MCP tools only — no text output.
Call synapse_set_project first, then synapse_add_node
for every module, file, and dependency, then synapse_link
to wire them together. Tools only.
```
